 {"id":"gt-6qld","title":"Daemon should auto-start as part of town bootstrap","description":"The Go daemon should be started automatically when the town boots up (mol-gastown-boot). Currently it requires manual 'gt daemon start'.

The daemon is critical infrastructure - it monitors the Deacon and nudges it when naked. Without it, the Deacon patrol loop doesn't run.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T13:16:52.000929-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:16:52.000929-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-6qyt1","title":"Refinery: event-driven merge queue","description":"## Problem
Refinery only acts on explicit requests, no queue management.

## ZFC-Compliant Solution
Add steps to `mol-refinery-patrol.formula.toml`:

```toml
[[step]]
id = "check-merge-requests"
title = "Process merge queue from inbox"
description = """
1. Check inbox for MERGE_READY messages: gt mail inbox
2. For each merge request:
   - Read branch name from message
   - Add to local queue (track in state.json or temp file)
3. Process queue in order:
   - git fetch origin
   - git checkout main && git pull
   - git merge --no-ff origin/polecat/<name>
   - If conflict: mail Mayor with details, skip
   - If success: git push && delete remote branch
4. For each merged branch:
   - gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "MERGED: polecat/<name>" -m "Branch merged successfully"
   - Delete the MERGE_READY mail
"""
depends_on = ["inbox-check"]
```

## Queue State
Refinery agent tracks queue in `state.json`:
```json
{
  "merge_queue": ["polecat/foo", "polecat/bar"],
  "last_merged": "polecat/baz"
}
```

## Why This Works
- Refinery Claude agent runs formula
- Agent handles conflicts with judgment
- Mail is coordination primitive
- State persists in agent's state.json

 {"id":"gt-6r18e.1","title":"Set GIT_AUTHOR_NAME per agent session","description":"Set GIT_AUTHOR_NAME to actor string (e.g., gastown/crew/joe) in agent session environment. GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL stays as workspace owner. Enables git log --author queries for agent work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:08:24.620264-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T16:09:30.244412-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T16:09:30.244412-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added GIT_AUTHOR_NAME export to all agent session spawn locations (13 files updated).  {"id":"gt-6r18e.1.1","title":"Clarify GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL handling for agents","description":"When setting GIT_AUTHOR_NAME per agent (gt-6r18e.1), also need to decide GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL:

Option A (proposed): Keep overseer's git email
- GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL = steve.yegge@gmail.com (from git config)
- This anchors ownership to the human
- Agents are tools of the overseer

Option B: Construct agent email
- GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL = gastown-crew-joe@gastown.local
- Fully separates agent identity
- May cause issues with GitHub/Bitbucket attribution

Recommendation: Option A - keeps human ownership clear while GIT_AUTHOR_NAME shows which agent did the work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-30T13:58:29.803201-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T16:48:19.865247-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T16:48:19.865247-08:00","close_reason":"Verified: Option A implemented and documented. GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL not exported, so git uses workspace owner's email from git config. Documentation in docs/federation.md lines 94-98 is accurate.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6r18e.1.1","depends_on_id":"gt-6r18e.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T13:58:29.805139-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}
 {"id":"gt-6r18e.2","title":"Default BD_ACTOR in beads create","description":"BUG: BD_ACTOR env var is only read in --no-db mode and daemon RPC path.  {"id":"gt-86w","title":"CLI: doctor diagnostics and auto-repair","description":"GGT completely lacks the doctor command which is critical for debugging.

Required Commands:
- gt doctor [<rig>] - Run diagnostic checks
- gt doctor --fix - Auto-repair common issues

Checks to Implement:
Workspace Level: Config validity, Mayor mailbox, Rig registry
Rig Level: Git state, clone health, witness/refinery presence, beads sync
Swarm Level: Stuck detection, zombie sessions, heartbeat health

PGT Reference: gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/dashboard_cmd.py","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:34.721484-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-i6k1","title":"Clean up duplicate patrol protos (gt-qflq, gt-iep9)","description":"There are duplicate protos for patrol molecules:

## Duplicates

| Old Proto | New Proto | Name |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| gt-qflq | mol-witness-patrol | mol-witness-patrol |
| gt-iep9 | mol-deacon-patrol | mol-deacon-patrol |

The old gt-* prefix protos were created before the formula cooking system.
The new mol-* prefix protos were created by `bd cook`.

## Action

1. Close or delete the old gt-* protos
2. Update any references to use the new mol-* protos
3. Verify `bd mol list` shows clean output

## Root Cause

The bd cook command uses the formula name as the proto ID (mol-*), 
not the project prefix (gt-*). This is probably correct behavior,
but means we have legacy protos to clean up.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:50:11.424341-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:55.510323-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:55.510323-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-i7wcn","title":"Polecat sessions terminate unexpectedly during work","description":"During swarm bd-784c, polecat sessions (Toast, Nux) terminated mid-task without completing their work.

**Observed:**
- Polecats were actively working (edits in progress, tests running)
- Sessions suddenly showed 'not running' in gt polecat status
- tmux sessions existed but were empty/reset
- Work was partially complete (some commits made, issue still open)

**Impact:**
- Lost work progress
- Required manual intervention to restart
- Unclear if work was saved/committed

**Suggestion:**
- Add session health monitoring
- Auto-restart on unexpected termination
- Log session exit reasons","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/toast","created_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:07.876275-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:07:52.213183-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:07:52.213183-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented session health monitoring and auto-restart for crashed polecats. Changes include: (1) daemon now proactively checks polecat session health and auto-restarts crashed sessions with work-on-hook, (2) added polecat support to lifecycle identity mapping, (3) added 'gt session check' command for manual health checking."}
 {"id":"gt-i9s7o","title":"Add tmux crash detection hooks","description":"Use tmux hooks to detect when agent panes die unexpectedly.

## tmux Hooks to Use
- pane-died: fires when a pane exits
- session-closed: fires when session ends

## Implementation
```bash
set-hook -g pane-died 'run-shell "gt log pane-died #{pane_id} #{pane_dead_status}"'
set-hook -g session-closed 'run-shell "gt log session-closed #{session_name}"'
```

## Integration
- gt spawn should set these hooks on agent sessions
- Distinguish expected exits (handoff, done) from crashes
- Capture last N lines of output on crash
- Record exit code/signal for forensics

## gt log crash command
- Parse pane-died events
- Show crash history
- Filter by agent, time range","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:29:40.899086-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.882716-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.882716-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

-{"id":"gt-xheo6","title":"Cross-rig worktree support for crew","description":"Enable crew workers to work on other rigs without losing identity.

**Problem**: When gastown/crew/joe needs to fix a beads bug, where do they work?
Current options are all problematic: mayor's rig (coordination, not coding), random
crew clone (conflicts), community repo (file locks), dogs (wrong abstraction).

**Solution**: Crew creates worktrees in target rigs with predictable paths.

Directory structure:
```
~/gt/<target-rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
~/gt/beads/crew/gastown-joe/     # joe from gastown working on beads
~/gt/gastown/crew/beads-wolf/    # wolf from beads working on gastown
```

Key principles:
- Identity preserved: BD_ACTOR stays gastown/crew/joe
- No conflicts: Each crew has own worktree
- No indirection: Direct work, no delegation to dogs
- Persistent: Worktree survives sessions (matches crew lifecycle)
- Dogs remain for Deacon's infrastructure work only

Commands:
- gt worktree <rig> - Create/enter worktree for rig
- gt worktree list - Show your worktrees  
- gt worktree remove <rig> - Clean up worktree","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:29.036817-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:29.036817-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-xheo6.1","title":"Implement gt worktree create command","description":"Create worktree in target rig for cross-rig work.

Usage: gt worktree <rig>

Behavior:
1. Detect current identity (gastown/crew/joe)
2. Compute worktree path: ~/gt/<rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
3. If worktree doesn't exist:
   - git worktree add <path> main
   - Set up .git config for identity preservation
4. cd to worktree (or print path for user to cd)
5. Preserve BD_ACTOR, GT_ROLE, etc.

Edge cases:
- Worktree already exists → just enter it
- Rig doesn't exist → error with helpful message
- No crew identity → error (must be crew to use this)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552114-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt worktree command for cross-rig work","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.1","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552745-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}
 {"id":"gt-xheo6.2","title":"Implement gt worktree list command","description":"List all worktrees owned by current crew member.

```
Cross-rig worktrees for gastown/crew/joe:

  beads     ~/gt/beads/crew/gastown-joe/     (clean)
  mayor     ~/gt/mayor/crew/gastown-joe/     (2 uncommitted)
```

Implementation:
- Scan all rigs in ~/gt/
- Check for crew/<source-rig>-<name>/ directories
- Show git status summary for each","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/capable","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:49.847297-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:03:18.471865-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:03:18.471865-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt worktree list command with role detection, cross-rig worktree scanning, and git status display","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.2","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:49.847843-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}
 {"id":"gt-xheo6.3","title":"Implement gt worktree remove command","description":"Remove a cross-rig worktree.

Usage: gt worktree remove <rig>

Behavior:
1. Check for uncommitted changes → warn/abort
2. git worktree remove <path>
3. Clean up directory if needed

Safety:
- Refuse if uncommitted changes (unless --force)
- Confirm before removal","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/toast","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857156-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:03:14.728163-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:03:14.728163-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt worktree remove command with safety checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.3","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857664-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}
